steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence reads: ‘He walked [word] to his seat without looking at the rest of us.’ The phrase ‘without looking at the rest of us’ shows Esteban moved without pausing or acknowledging anyone. The blank must capture that quality of movement.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Delightedly': means with happiness or joy. Esteban arrived soaking wet with his head down — the scene conveys no joy. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Truthfully': means in an honest manner. This applies to speech or conduct, not to physical movement from one point to another. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Directly': means in a straight line, without stopping or detouring. ‘He walked directly to his seat without looking at the rest of us’ matches the image of Esteban moving straight ahead and ignoring everyone around him. ✓
  • Choice D — 'Formally': means in a stiff or ceremonious way. Nothing in the passage suggests ceremony — he is soaking wet and keeping his head down. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
